blocks.content: Friday came and I did not go to Marcus's spot. I did not text Jonathan about it. I did homework and stayed in and let my phone be quiet.
Saturday morning. I was on the front steps with a book I wasn't reading when Mr. Cole came out of his apartment and stood there for a second, looking at nothing in particular.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: You been out here a while.
He sat down on the step above me. Not next to me — above. The way someone sits when they're not trying to crowd you but they're not going anywhere either.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: Your brother told me you had a bad week.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: He ask permission to tell me?
We sat. A car went by. Someone across the street called someone else's name.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: And you and Jonathan had a falling out about it.
He didn't push the something like that. That was the thing about Mr. Cole — he waited for the actual sentence, not the first version of it.
